patch.name: Peer Review Crossfire
patch.author: timepoint-pro
patch.description: Grant review panel simulation with branching panel composition counterfactuals

panel_members.description: Senior administrator balancing institutional priorities, budget realities, and strategic alignment. Knows the real budget picture and political considerations that other reviewers don't see.
Established scientist with deep domain expertise. Applies rigorous methodological standards and carries institutional memory of past failed grants in similar areas.
Early-career researcher who champions novel approaches and emerging research directions. Brings awareness of cutting-edge methods but less institutional experience.
Regulatory specialist focused on IRB compliance, data management plans, budget justification rules, and institutional risk exposure.
Outside expert bringing fresh perspective and awareness of how other institutions handle similar proposals. Less entangled in internal politics.

phases.name: Initial Review
phases.description: Panel members present individual scores and first impressions. Scoring criteria discussed. Each reviewer shares their preliminary rating with brief rationale.

phases.name: Deliberation
phases.description: Open debate on application merits. Arguments and counter-arguments. Coalition formation as reviewers align on key issues. Information asymmetries begin to surface as private knowledge is selectively revealed.

phases.name: Final Consensus
phases.description: Formal voting on recommendation. Dissenting opinions recorded. Final scores tabulated. Recommendation letter drafted with majority and minority views.

scientific_merit.description: Quality of research question, methodology, and theoretical framework

innovation.description: Novelty of approach, potential for breakthrough findings

feasibility.description: Realistic timeline, appropriate methods, qualified team

broader_impact.description: Societal relevance, training opportunities, outreach plans

diverse_panel.description: Default panel composition with varied biases (institutional_balance, scientific_rigor, innovation, compliance, peer_assessment). Represents typical review panel diversity.
diverse_panel.expected_dynamics: Balanced debate with competing priorities. Innovation advocate counters skeptic. Compliance officer raises risk flags. External reviewer provides calibration. Program Director mediates.

excellence_only.description: All panelists share scientific_rigor bias. Simulates a panel composed entirely of established researchers focused on methodological excellence.
excellence_only.expected_dynamics: Convergent criticism on methodology. Less attention to innovation or broader impact. Higher rejection rate. Groupthink risk. Budget considerations minimized.

schema.name: string — Institution name
schema.entity_type: string — e.g., R1 university, liberal arts college, research institute
schema.history: string — Relevant funding and research history

default.name: Midwest State University
default.mission: Advancing knowledge through inclusive research and education
default.history: Moderate NIH/NSF funding portfolio. Two prior awards in related area, one not renewed.

grant_opportunity.description: Details of the funding opportunity being reviewed

schema.funder: string — Funding agency (e.g., NIH, NSF, DOE)
schema.amount: number — Requested funding amount in USD

application_summary.description: Summary of the grant application under review

schema.key_claims: array — Central claims the proposal makes

default.narrative: Proposes novel deep learning architecture for multi-omics integration in rare disease diagnosis. Combines transformer-based feature extraction with causal inference framework. Plans to validate on three rare disease cohorts with clinical partners.
default.budget_justification: PI salary (20%), one postdoc, two grad students, cloud computing costs, clinical partner subawards. Equipment: GPU cluster for model training.
key_claims: Novel architecture outperforms existing methods by 15% on benchmark datasets
Causal framework enables interpretable predictions unlike black-box alternatives
Clinical validation pipeline ensures translational relevance
Open-source toolkit will benefit broader rare disease research community

expected_outputs.per_reviewer_scores: Individual scores (1-10) per criterion with written rationale for each panelist
expected_outputs.panel_consensus: Final recommendation: fund, fund_with_revisions, not_recommended, or defer
expected_outputs.decision_arguments: Key arguments that drove the final recommendation, ranked by influence
expected_outputs.information_asymmetry_report: Analysis of how private knowledge (budget constraints, rejection history) affected the deliberation outcome
expected_outputs.counterfactual_comparison: Side-by-side outcomes from diverse_panel vs excellence_only branches
